Hey guys, so this is just serving as an intro to The setter calls that you'll see me doing and what I want to do with this intro is just give you a bit of Behind-the-scenes stuff. So you'll be viewing set calls that were a success So I was successfully able to book appointments into John's schedule where he then closes successfully Clientele for the HTTP program now. It's not all sun shines rainbows and unicorns in the process So I want to give you a realistic picture or the unfiltered version of what a lead sheet really looks like over a process of a campaign So right here I have our very first HTTP lead campaign that we launched in September and we did this for about a month. So The four weeks worth of leads so in three to four weeks We've accumulated 150 leads and you'll notice is a lot of red and white. So those are all dead ends So the reds are those that we got in touch with and they either straight up rejected me Didn't even want to do the interview phone call or during that interview or that pre-screen process. So that's set or call You know just wasn't able to you know overcome their obstacles in an effective manner to get them to sign up So these are just all dead ends and as you can see there are a lot of them But we also have you know in these blues so these blues are those who where I did have a successful set close They went to see John and he successfully closed them and they joined the program So of 150 leads we had 21 sign up for our HTTP Program and we had three greens so the greens were those who actually they I successfully closed the set They put down their deposit for the appointment But then when they met with John the program wasn't the right fit But they signed on to a different service through us So I consider that you know 24 success stories out of the hundred and fifty so just some math because we love math If you think about it these leads on average So they were valued at $200 spent per lead and each close was worth a minimum of $2,000 So that means for every 10 leads for every one package you sell HTTP Especially if it's on the low end of the price point, which would be that $2,000 mark. That's worth 10 leads So you've covered what you've spent for 10 leads So so let's see we got 21 sign up for HTTP out of 150 So that's 14 percent Close on all of our appointments after everyone, you know the no shows the shows so 14 percent out of the hundred and fifty leads Is what we ended up with now just to translate that into dollars so our 21 HTTP clients from that first campaign, you know times The price point where we were selling it out, which is around 2000 that adds up to $42,000 for that campaign and $42,000 in one month's time pretty good chunk of change pretty significant So just keep that in mind and keep the big picture in mind as you're doing this and I'll show you our current Lead sheet So we just started another another campaign We've been running it for two weeks including the Thanksgiving holiday So that already cuts out a few days and that first week was brutal for me I didn't close a single one and you know I follow like I thought the same tactics that you see in the success calls I followed those same tactics the script outs provides is phenomenal So it asks the right questions in the right order What I would suggest to you and I hope what you pick up from these videos is my use of tone Being able to adapt the questions to the person the situation So it has a genuine touch to it being genuine across the phone is super important because you are laying down that first impression for your facility So if the car if um, you know the lead Isn't picking up on the high energy vibes that you're putting out then that phone It's going to affect the tone of the phone call and you may come across as less convincing if you're reading right off of a script And if you're not confident and if you have no personality across the phone So all those things are really important and the most important piece is not letting these reds or these yellows which are you know Like no answers Don't let that get to you The bigger picture is know that for every lead that you close. It's worth 10 Okay, at least and that's at the low end of the price point So hope you guys get a lot out of the videos that I was able to put together for you Another thing too is you'll notice that I suffer a lot of technical difficulties in the calls So I think it's a really authentic view of what happens like when your system crashes and some backup things that you can do So you make sure that you get people booked into the schedule that you're taking the deposits and that on the other end of the line They don't even know that you're dealing with a bunch of computer issues So hope all this stuff is useful for you guys and you know Bottom line is keep the call volume up push hard with those leads Keep the lead sheet as updated as possible. Everyone gets at least three calls a day do it Alex says it really does work But yes, you are going to see a lot of red and have a few horror stories, but it's worth it for those successes All right.
